title: scrophularia variegata subsp. depauperata
intro: Scrophularia variegata subsp. depauperata, also known as the poor man's rope, is a subspecies of the figwort plant, which is a flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are said to aid in the treatment of various health conditions. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system, making it a sought-after remedy in herbal medicine. Scrophularia variegata subsp. depauperata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusement, and is often used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
